title: Timing and Spacing of Immunobiologics | Vaccines & Immunizations | CDC
text:

Use of combination vaccines can reduce the number of injections patients receive and alleviate concern associated with the number of injections 29 37 38 . Studies have demonstrated that parents and providers might be uncomfortable with multiple injections during single visits 39 40 41 . Potential advantages of combination vaccines include: 
Improved vaccine coverage rates. 42 
Timely vaccination coverage for children who are behind in the schedule. 43 44 
Reduced shipping and stocking costs. 
Reduced costs for extra health care visits necessitated by deferral of vaccination. 
Facilitation of additional new vaccines into vaccination programs. 
Potential disadvantages of combination vaccines include the following: 
Adverse events that might occur more frequently after administration of a combination vaccine compared with administration of separate antigens at the same visit, such as fever that occurs with the combination MMRV vaccine and combination DTaP-HepB-IPV vaccine 45 46 . 
Confusion and uncertainty about selection of vaccine combinations and schedules for subsequent doses, especially when vaccinations are given by multiple providers who might be using different products. 
Reduced pathogen coverage if the combination product covers fewer types of one particular vaccine-preventable diseas
